name: University of Cambridge
notes: Cambridge students apply to Engineering (H100) and specialise into Mechanical Engineering from Year 3. The first two years cover all engineering disciplines. ENGAA entrance assessment required; interview at college. World-leading thermodynamics, fluid mechanics and structural mechanics groups.

name: University of Oxford
notes: Oxford Engineering Science (H100) students specialise in mechanical stream from Year 3. PAT (Physics Aptitude Test) required at application. Interview required. Mathematics and Physics A-levels essential.

name: Imperial College London
notes: UCAS 2026 verified title match. World-renowned for thermofluids, solid mechanics and advanced materials. Strong employer links to Rolls-Royce, BP and aerospace sector. A* in Maths expected; Physics required.

name: Loughborough University
notes: UCAS 2026 verified title match. Loughborough's Mechanical and Manufacturing Engineering is one of the UK's highest-ranked. Strong Formula SAE, motorsport and aerospace connections; graduates at McLaren, Rolls-Royce, Red Bull Racing and JLR. Placement year option highly recommended.

name: University of Bath
notes: UCAS 2026 verified title match. Bath consistently ranks top 5 for Mechanical Engineering nationally. Integrated placement year (Semester 5) is a major differentiator; placement employers include Rolls-Royce, Dyson, JLR and BAE Systems. AAA required; Mathematics and Physics essential.

name: University of Bristol
notes: UCAS 2026 verified title match. Bristol has strong aerospace and advanced materials links (Rolls-Royce, Airbus UK is based near Bristol). Excellent research facilities including a hypersonic wind tunnel. Mathematics and Physics required.

name: University of Warwick
notes: UCAS 2026 verified title match. Warwick Manufacturing Group (WMG) is one of the world's leading automotive and manufacturing research groups; JLR, BMW and Tata Motors are close partners. Strong electric vehicle research. Mathematics and Physics required.

name: University of Manchester
notes: UCAS 2026 verified title match. Manchester's graphene expertise feeds directly into advanced materials modules. Strong fluid mechanics research group. Mathematics required; Physics strongly recommended.

name: University of Nottingham
notes: UCAS 2026 verified title match. Rolls-Royce University Technology Centre at Nottingham focuses on advanced manufacturing and gas turbines. Excellent employer links in the East Midlands manufacturing corridor. Mathematics required at A-level.

name: University of Southampton
notes: UCAS 2026 verified title match. Southampton is world-leading in fluid dynamics and acoustic engineering. The ship hydrodynamics laboratory and wind tunnels provide exceptional experimental facilities. Strong links to Rolls-Royce and Lloyd's Register. Mathematics required at A-level.

name: University of Sheffield
notes: UCAS 2026 verified title match. Sheffield's AMRC (Advanced Manufacturing Research Centre) with Boeing is one of the UK's premier manufacturing research centres. Strong aerospace, automotive and advanced manufacturing connections. Excellent industry partnerships.

name: University of Leeds
notes: UCAS 2026 verified title match. Leeds Mechanical Engineering is strong in tribology, thermodynamics and process equipment. Good industrial placement opportunities. Mathematics required at A-level; Physics recommended.
